description: Open-source Chrome extension that converts a single profile page into a clean PDF or DOCX file via the user's own Vision LLM API key (BYOK). Manifest V3, MIT-licensed. The extension runs entirely in the user's browser; the project operates no server. The extension is a generic page-to-document converter — it does not target any specific website, does not include site-specific selectors, and does not specify a destination for the produced file. What the user does with the file after it downloads is outside the project's scope.
globalQualityGates: Story-scoped commits — no unrelated files staged.
Verify build outputs in dist/ before passes=true.
Manifest V3 compliant: no inline scripts, no eval, no remote code execution, MV3 service worker pattern.
TypeScript strict mode; no `any` outside controlled boundaries.
BYOK keys never leave the user's browser to a third-party server controlled by this project. Encryption at rest in chrome.storage.local via WebCrypto AES-GCM.
No site-specific selectors, no target-website list, no hardcoded URL allowlist beyond `<all_urls>`. The extraction is a pure prompt-driven Vision LLM call.

No copy or marketing language anywhere (README / DISCLAIMER / PRIVACY / store listing / popup UI / extracted-file metadata) directs the user toward any specific destination platform, ATS, CRM, or service.
UI stories visually verified by loading dist/ as unpacked extension.

userStories.title: Vite + CRXJS + React + TS scaffold
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I need a working dev environment with hot-reload so I can iterate fast.
acceptanceCriteria: package.json with vite + @crxjs/vite-plugin + react + react-dom + typescript + zod
tsconfig.json strict mode + DOM lib + chrome types
vite.config.ts wires CRXJS plugin pointing at src/manifest.json
src/manifest.json declares Manifest V3 with name 'clipcv' / version 0.1.0 / icons placeholder
npm run dev starts dev server with HMR
npm run build emits dist/ with manifest.json + bundled scripts
dist/ loads in chrome://extensions as unpacked without errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Scaffold landed: vite 8 + @crxjs/vite-plugin 2.4 + react 18.3 + typescript 5.9 strict + zod 4. `npm run build` produces dist/manifest.json (MV3, name=clipcv v0.1.0) + service-worker-loader.js + assets/service-worker bundle + icons/{16,48,128}.png — all non-zero. Typecheck clean; dev server boots with HMR.

userStories.title: Popup UI shell + service worker hello world
userStories.description: Extension toolbar icon opens a popup with a placeholder UI; background service worker logs lifecycle events.
acceptanceCriteria: src/popup/popup.html + popup.tsx render a minimal Tailwind shell with name + a disabled placeholder Capture button
src/background/service-worker.ts logs install/activate/runtime events at debug level
manifest.json action.default_popup points at popup.html
manifest.json background.service_worker points at the compiled service-worker file
Popup opens on toolbar click and shows the shell
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser by loading unpacked
userStories.notes: Tailwind v4 + @tailwindcss/vite installed; popup.html + main.tsx + Popup.tsx + popup.css render the shell with disabled Capture button; manifest.action.default_popup wired; SW logs onInstalled/onStartup/onSuspend at debug level. Build emits dist/manifest.json with action block, dist/src/popup/popup.html, popup-*.css (8.77 KB Tailwind), popup.html-*.js (141 KB React bundle). 18/18 programmatic AC checks PASS; no inline scripts / eval / Function ctor in dist.

userStories.title: BYOK config page with encrypted local storage
userStories.description: User enters LLM provider, base URL, model, and API key. Stored encrypted at rest in chrome.storage.local.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings page (chrome://options for clipcv) with fields: provider (preset list + 'OpenAI-compatible custom'), base_url, model, api_key, default output format (pdf | docx | both)
API key encrypted with WebCrypto AES-GCM using a random 256-bit key per install (key + IV stored alongside ciphertext in chrome.storage.local; key never sent over the network)
chrome.storage.local persists ciphertext + IV + the encryption key; never stores plaintext api_key
Form validation: api_key required, base_url is a URL
'Test connection' button: a 1-token completion call returning latency_ms or a typed error code (auth_failed / timeout / model_not_found / bad_request / rate_limited / unknown)
Test errors masked of any text matching `sk-[A-Za-z0-9]{16,}` or `Bearer [A-Za-z0-9._-]{20,}` patterns before display
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Options page wired via manifest.options_page + storage permission. WebCrypto AES-GCM module (src/lib/crypto/byok-crypto.ts) generates a per-install 256-bit raw key on first save and reuses it; ciphertext + IV + raw key all live in chrome.storage.local under 'byok_settings'. Plaintext API key never persisted. src/lib/storage/byok-store.ts encapsulates load/save/clear. Test connection helper (src/lib/llm/test-connection.ts) issues a 1-token /chat/completions POST with 15s timeout and maps status → typed error code (auth_failed / timeout / model_not_found / bad_request / rate_limited / unknown). src/lib/util/scrub-secrets.ts scrubs sk-*/Bearer-* before display. Form validates non-empty API key + http(s) base URL + non-empty model. Build emits dist/manifest.json with options_page + permissions:[storage], dist/src/options/options.html (no inline JS), dist/assets/options.html-*.js (9.33 KB) + jsx-runtime-*.js (140 KB shared). 21/21 programmatic AC checks PASS; no eval / new Function / inline scripts in dist; §6.4 forbidden-copy grep over src/options + src/lib clean. Also fixed a pre-existing vite.config.ts alias bug (URL.pathname kept %20 in repo paths with spaces) by decodeURIComponent — required to make @/* alias resolve under 'OptiTalent Nan V1/clipcv'.

userStories.title: Floating Capture button content script injection
userStories.description: Inject a small floating button so the user can click it to start capture on the current page.
acceptanceCriteria: src/content/inject.tsx mounts a shadow-DOM root with a floating button anchored bottom-right
manifest.json content_scripts matches `<all_urls>`
Idempotent: button never re-injects if shadow root already exists
Shadow DOM isolates styling so host page CSS does not bleed in or out
Click sends a typed message to background/service-worker via chrome.runtime.sendMessage with the active tab's URL
Hidden when disclaimer not yet accepted (gated by US-012)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Content script entry src/content/inject.tsx mounts a shadow-DOM host (id=clipcv-floating-host) on document.documentElement, idempotent via getElementById guard. Shadow root receives an inlined <style> with the build-time-processed Tailwind v4 stylesheet (?inline import) so host-page CSS cannot bleed in or out. Floating <button> is fixed bottom-4 right-4 with z-index 2147483647; hidden until chrome.storage.local.disclaimer_acceptance.{accepted:true, version: <manifest.version>} matches the running build (gate stub at src/lib/storage/disclaimer-store.ts; US-012 lands the modal that writes it). chrome.storage.onChanged listener flips the gate live without needing a reload. Click dispatches a typed {kind:'capture-request', url:window.location.href, pageTitle:document.title} via chrome.runtime.sendMessage; service worker validates the kind via a discriminated-union type guard (src/lib/messaging/messages.ts) — unknown kinds return {kind:'error', reason:'unknown_kind'}, capture-request returns {kind:'capture-request-ack', receivedAt}. manifest.content_scripts matches `<all_urls>`, run_at=document_idle, all_frames=false. CRXJS emits a tiny loader script + a web_accessible_resources bundle for the actual content code (no inline JS). Build output: dist/assets/inject.tsx-*.js (13.34 KB) with Tailwind CSS string inlined; no separate inject CSS file. 26/26 programmatic AC checks PASS (incl. shadow-root creation, idempotency guard, typed message dispatch, SW handler unknown-kind rejection, no eval / no new Function / no inline scripts); §6.4 forbidden-copy grep over src/content + new src/lib paths clean.

userStories.title: Capture viewport screenshot + visible DOM HTML
userStories.description: On Capture click, snapshot the viewport and serialize the visible DOM tree.
acceptanceCriteria: service worker handles capture-request message
Calls chrome.tabs.captureVisibleTab() → base64 PNG (downsample to <= 1280px width to control LLM token cost)
Sends back to content script which serializes document.body.innerHTML stripped of `<script>` / `<style>` / `<noscript>` and any element with attribute `aria-hidden="true"`
Returns {screenshot_b64, html, page_url, page_title, captured_at} payload
Total payload size capped at 1MB; over the cap, drops DOM and uses screenshot only
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Service worker handles capture-request via async sendResponse (returns true to keep the channel open) — chrome.tabs.captureVisibleTab → fetch(dataURL) → createImageBitmap → if width > 1280 px, OffscreenCanvas downsample + FileReader.readAsDataURL → returns {kind:'capture-screenshot', screenshot_b64, captured_at}. Errors map to {kind:'error', reason:'screenshot_failed', detail}. src/lib/capture/dom-serialize.ts uses cloneNode(true) + TreeWalker(SHOW_ELEMENT) to detach SCRIPT/STYLE/NOSCRIPT and aria-hidden="true" subtrees, then returns innerHTML. src/lib/capture/payload.ts caps the JSON-serialized UTF-8 payload at 1_000_000 bytes — over the cap, html is dropped (screenshot retained as primary signal). src/content/capture.ts orchestrates: sendMessage → screenshot from SW + serialize DOM locally → compose {screenshot_b64, html, page_url, page_title, captured_at} → applyPayloadSizeCap. FloatingButton click handler uses requestCapture and logs a payload summary at debug level (full LLM pipeline lands in US-008..US-011). manifest.host_permissions adds <all_urls> (required for captureVisibleTab on arbitrary tabs; activeTab cannot be granted via content-script gestures). Build emits dist/assets/service-worker.ts-*.js (1.60 KB) with captureVisibleTab + OffscreenCanvas + createImageBitmap + FileReader + capture-screenshot/screenshot_failed kinds; dist/assets/inject.tsx-*.js (14.45 KB) with cloneNode + TreeWalker + SCRIPT/STYLE/NOSCRIPT/aria-hidden filters + 1_000_000 cap + TextEncoder. 33/33 programmatic AC checks PASS (manifest/SW/content bundle structural greps + payload-size-cap pure-logic round-trip in node); no eval / new Function / inline scripts in dist; §6.4 forbidden-copy grep over the new src files clean. Typecheck clean.

userStories.title: Vision LLM provider abstraction
userStories.description: Single client supporting any OpenAI-compatible Vision API.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/llm/client.ts: callVisionLLM({provider, baseUrl, apiKey, model, screenshot_b64, html, prompt}) → string
OpenAI-compatible /chat/completions with vision messages array (image_url base64 + text)
Default timeout 30s; one retry on 5xx; no retry on 4xx
Provider-specific quirks isolated in adapter layer (each adapter exports the same signature)
Returns the raw assistant content string; parsing is downstream
Throws typed Error subclasses: AuthError, TimeoutError, RateLimitedError, BadRequestError, UnknownLlmError
Unit tests cover happy path + timeout + auth error + rate limit
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: callVisionLLM lives in src/lib/llm/client.ts, dispatches via ADAPTERS[provider] in src/lib/llm/adapters.ts (single OpenAI-compatible builder shared by openai/openrouter/custom — adapter layer is the home for future per-provider quirks like Anthropic /v1/messages). Builds /chat/completions with messages:[{role:'user',content:[{type:'text'},{type:'image_url',image_url:{url:'data:image/png;base64,...'}}]}], 30s default timeout via AbortController, single retry on 5xx, no retry on 4xx. Throws AuthError/TimeoutError/RateLimitedError/BadRequestError/UnknownLlmError (src/lib/llm/errors.ts) — every thrown message scrubbed via scrubSecrets. vitest 4.1.5 wired (vitest.config.ts mirrors vite.config.ts decodeURIComponent alias trick), `npm test` script added; 13/13 tests pass covering happy path, timeout, 401/403 → AuthError, 429 → RateLimitedError, 400 → BadRequestError, 5xx retries exactly once + recovers, 5xx-then-5xx → UnknownLlmError, sk-* scrubbing in error messages, malformed 200 response → UnknownLlmError, baseUrl trailing-slash join, raw-base64-without-data-prefix handling, request shape (URL + headers + body parts). typecheck + build clean; new src/lib/llm/* never imports a chrome.* API or DOM — runs in any fetch-capable environment incl. vitest's node env. No §6.4 forbidden copy in new files. Not yet wired into any extension entry — the integration into the floating-button click pipeline lands in US-011.

userStories.title: Zod schema for ExtractedProfile
userStories.description: Strict schema for the structured profile data the model returns.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/schema/profile.ts exports a z.object: name, email?, phone?, current_title?, current_company?, location?, summary?, experiences[], educations[], skills[], certifications[], languages[]
experiences[] item: {company, title, start_date?, end_date?, location?, bullets: string[]}
educations[] item: {institution, degree?, field?, start_date?, end_date?, location?}
All date fields are free-form strings (model returns whatever the page shows; we do not normalize)
Schema rejects unknown top-level keys; required minimum is `name`
Export type ExtractedProfile = z.infer<typeof schema>
Unit tests with golden fixture pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Schema lives in src/lib/schema/profile.ts as ExtractedProfileSchema (top-level z.strictObject — rejects unknown keys), with name as the sole required field. Optional primitives (email/phone/current_title/current_company/location/summary) use z.string().min(1).optional(); arrays (experiences/educations/skills/certifications/languages) use .default([]) so the model may omit them and still produce a valid output with empty arrays. ExperienceItemSchema requires {company, title} + optional {start_date, end_date, location} (free-form strings, no normalization) + bullets:string[].default([]); EducationItemSchema requires {institution} + optional {degree, field, start_date, end_date, location}. Exports ExtractedProfile = z.infer<typeof ExtractedProfileSchema> plus ExperienceItem / EducationItem types for downstream renderers (US-009/US-010). Tests in src/lib/schema/__tests__/profile.test.ts cover golden-fixture parse, minimal-name-only parse with array defaults, missing-name rejection, unknown-top-level-key rejection (asserts the zod 4 'unrecognized_keys' issue.keys array contains the offending field), nested-required-field rejection on experiences[*].company/title, empty-string name rejection, free-form date round-trip, and bullets[]-default omission. `npm run typecheck` clean, `npm test` 21 passed (8 new + 13 existing LLM-client), `npm run build` produced dist/ — schema not yet wired into any entry (the extract pipeline binds to it in US-008). Note: zod 4 strict-mode rejection surfaces as code:'unrecognized_keys' with empty path[] and a keys[] array, NOT a path-targeted issue — different from zod 3.

userStories.title: Vision extraction prompt + JSON validation pipeline
userStories.description: Compose the LLM prompt requesting Zod-shaped JSON, validate the response, return ExtractedProfile or a typed error.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/extract/vision-extract.ts: extractProfile({screenshot_b64, html}) → ExtractedProfile
Prompt instructs the model to ignore navigation/ads/footer/sidebar and only output the primary subject's structured data
Prompt requests `response_format: json_object` (and includes JSON schema text for providers that ignore the API field)
Zod validates the response; on parse failure, retry once with a stricter prompt that includes the schema again
Errors surfaced as typed: AUTH | TIMEOUT | NO_PROFILE_DETECTED | SCHEMA_MISMATCH | UNKNOWN_LLM
Unit tests with golden fixture: parse success, schema mismatch, no profile detected
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: src/lib/extract/vision-extract.ts exposes extractProfile({provider,baseUrl,apiKey,model,screenshot_b64,html,timeoutMs?,callImpl?,fetchImpl?}) → ExtractedProfile. Uses callVisionLLM with responseFormatJson=true and includes the JSON schema text inline in the prompt for providers that ignore the API field. Two-attempt loop: base prompt (instructs model to ignore navigation/ads/footer/sidebar/related/comments/secondary-people) → strict prompt on parse failure (re-includes schema; demands raw JSON object first/last char). parseJsonLoose tolerates direct JSON, ```json``` code fences, and prose-wrapped {...} blocks. Sentinel {"_no_profile": true} short-circuits to ExtractError('NO_PROFILE_DETECTED'). LLM transport errors mapped: AuthError→AUTH, TimeoutError→TIMEOUT, RateLimited/BadRequest/Unknown→UNKNOWN_LLM. Two-strikes-and-throw → ExtractError('SCHEMA_MISMATCH', summarised zod issues). Adapter + client extended with optional responseFormatJson?: boolean (only set under exactOptionalPropertyTypes when true) — adapter emits OpenAI 'response_format':{'type':'json_object'} on the request body when the flag is set. 14 new tests in src/lib/extract/__tests__/vision-extract.test.ts covering: golden-fixture happy path, code-fence strip, embedded-block extract, NO_PROFILE_DETECTED sentinel, retry-on-non-JSON-recovers, two-failure SCHEMA_MISMATCH, missing-name SCHEMA_MISMATCH, unknown-top-level-key strict rejection, AUTH/TIMEOUT/UNKNOWN_LLM (incl. RateLimited collapse) mapping, responseFormatJson + creds + timeoutMs pass-through, base-prompt structural assertions (navigation/ads/footer/sidebar/_no_profile/experiences/educations). `npm run typecheck` clean, `npm test` 35/35 pass across 3 files (14 new + 13 LLM client + 8 schema), `npm run build` produces dist/ unchanged from US-007 (extract not yet bundled — wired into the capture pipeline in US-011). §6.4 forbidden-copy grep over src/lib/extract clean (no ats/crm/recruiter/workflow/optitalent/linkedin/indeed/naukri); no eval / new Function. Note: zod 4 issue.path is PropertyKey[] (string|number|symbol), so the issue summariser maps via String(p) before join — different from zod 3.

userStories.title: PDF renderer
userStories.description: Render an ExtractedProfile to a clean PDF.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/render/pdf.ts uses pdf-lib (deterministic, no external server)
Page size: A4. Single font family (Helvetica). Body 11pt, section heading 14pt bold
Content order: Header (name + contact) → Summary → Experience → Education → Skills → Certifications → Languages
Optional sections omitted entirely when empty (no empty headings)
Filename: `{LastFirst_or_Unknown}_{YYYYMMDD}.pdf`
Unit test produces a valid PDF for the fixture and asserts non-zero byte size and PDF magic header
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: renderPdf in src/lib/render/pdf.ts uses pdf-lib 1.17.1 with StandardFonts.Helvetica + HelveticaBold (single family per AC); A4 595.28×841.89pt, 50pt margin, body 11pt, headings 14pt bold, name 20pt bold; section gating ensures empty optional fields produce zero output (no empty headings). Sections render in the AC-mandated order: header (name + title-company + location|email|phone) → Summary → Experience (bold title-company line + date line + indented bullets) → Education (bold institution + degree+field+location line + date line) → Skills → Certifications → Languages (each as comma-joined list). Multi-page support via `ensureSpace` cursor that adds a new page when y < margin. Filename helper extracted to src/lib/render/filename.ts (will be reused by US-010 DOCX) — `formatFilename(name, now, ext)` produces `{Last_First_or_Unknown}_{YYYYMMDD}.{ext}` with §10.1 sanitization (control chars + path separators stripped via charcode iteration to dodge JSON-string-escape pitfalls in tooling, `..` parent-dir removed, 64-char cap, `Unknown` fallback when empty). Times come from `options.now?: Date` so the PDF metadata + filename are deterministic in tests; `setCreationDate`/`setModificationDate` pinned for the same reason. 12 unit tests across 2 files (4 in pdf.test.ts: golden-fixture happy path with magic-header + size>0, minimal-name-only profile, multi-page paginate-don't-crash; 8 in filename.test.ts: two-token Last_First, single-token, undefined/empty fallback, separator+control-char strip, post-sanitize-empty fallback, 64-char cap, ext switch, YYYYMMDD zero-pad). `npm run typecheck` clean, `npm test` 48/48 pass across 5 files (12 new render specs + 14 extract + 13 LLM client + 8 schema + 1 baseline), `npm run build` produces dist/ unchanged from US-008 (renderer not yet bundled into any extension entry — wired into the click pipeline in US-011). §6.4 forbidden-copy grep over src/lib/render clean (no ats/crm/recruiter/recruiting/workflow/optitalent/linkedin/indeed/naukri); no eval / new Function. pdf.ts is 274 LOC (under §9 300-LOC ceiling) after extracting filename.ts. Note: the Write tool interprets `\u0000` etc. in source strings as actual control bytes, corrupting any literal control-char regex; the workaround is char-code iteration (`for (const ch of …) ch.charCodeAt(0) < 0x20`) instead of `/[\u0000-\u001f]/`.

userStories.title: DOCX renderer
userStories.description: Same data → DOCX. User picks PDF / DOCX / both in settings.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/render/docx.ts uses the `docx` npm package
Same content order as PDF (US-009)
Settings selector controls which renderer(s) run on capture
Filename convention identical to PDF, .docx extension
Unit test produces a valid DOCX with non-zero size
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: renderDocx in src/lib/render/docx.ts uses docx@9.6.1 (Document + Paragraph + TextRun + HeadingLevel + Packer.toBlob) to emit a {bytes:Uint8Array, filename}-shape result that mirrors the PDF renderer's contract. Section order matches US-009 PDF: Header (name 20pt + title-company line + location|email|phone) → Summary → Experience (bold title-company + date line + indented `* bullet` paragraphs at 360-twip indent) → Education (bold institution + degree+field+location + date line) → Skills → Certifications → Languages — every optional section gated on populated check so empty inputs produce zero output (no empty headings). Filename via shared formatFilename(name, now, 'docx') from US-009 — pattern `{Last_First_or_Unknown}_{YYYYMMDD}.docx`. options.now?: Date is the determinism seam (creator metadata is stamped 'clipcv'). Settings selector wired via new src/lib/render/dispatch.ts:renderForOutput(profile, output, opts) which reads the OutputFormat ('pdf' | 'docx' | 'both') already persisted by US-003's BYOK store and returns RenderedFile[] with {bytes, filename, mimeType} per file. US-011 imports this dispatcher to honour the user's output preference. Tests: 6 in docx.test.ts (golden-fixture happy path with PK\x03\x04 ZIP magic + size>0, filename pattern, minimal-name-only profile, Unknown-literal-name passthrough, JSZip crack-open of word/document.xml asserting Jane Doe + Heading2 + each section label is rendered, minimal-profile asserts NO empty section headings emitted) + 4 in dispatch.test.ts (output='pdf' emits 1 PDF with application/pdf mime, output='docx' emits 1 DOCX with the openxml mime, output='both' emits 2 files in pdf-then-docx order, options.now omission falls back to current Date with YYYYMMDD bracketed by before/after stamps). `npm run typecheck` clean, `npm test` 58/58 pass across 7 files (10 new + 48 prior), `npm run build` produces dist/ (manifest 1.20 KB, popup/options/SW unchanged byte-for-byte, inject.tsx-* +60 B due to build hash variance — render layer is NOT yet imported by any extension entry; consumer wiring lands in US-011, by design). §6.4 forbidden-copy grep over src/lib/render clean (no ats/crm/recruiter/recruiting/workflow/optitalent/linkedin/indeed/naukri); no eval / new Function. docx.ts is 249 LOC, dispatch.ts is 48 LOC — both under §9 300-LOC ceiling. Note: docx@9.6.1 has @types/node in `dependencies` (not devDependencies) for type augmentation; runtime tree pulls jszip + nanoid + xml-js + hash.js — Vite bundles all of these for the browser. Packer.toBlob → blob.arrayBuffer → Uint8Array works in BOTH the MV3 service worker (browser Blob) AND vitest's node>=18 env (global Blob with arrayBuffer); no need for the Node-only Packer.toBuffer code path.

userStories.title: End-to-end capture flow + download trigger
userStories.description: Click button → spinner → extract → render → download.
acceptanceCriteria: Floating button click runs the full pipeline with a loading state and a Cancel option
On success, chrome.downloads.download triggers the browser's save dialog
On error, an in-page toast surfaces a user-friendly message keyed off the error enum (no raw stack traces, no API key fragments)
Pipeline completes under 30s on average for a typical profile page
If both PDF and DOCX selected, both files download (browser save dialog twice)
Verify end-to-end on a sample profile page (e.g. a public blog 'About the author' page)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: End-to-end pipeline lives in src/lib/pipeline/run-pipeline.ts (orchestrator) + src/lib/pipeline/user-messages.ts (toast copy). runPipeline takes a CapturePayload + AbortSignal + onStage callback and walks extracting → rendering → downloading → done; every stage boundary checks the abort signal so a Cancel click between stages surfaces as PipelineError('CANCELLED'); abort during the in-flight LLM fetch is also collapsed to CANCELLED rather than the underlying error. ExtractError → PipelineError mapping preserves the AC-mandated codes (AUTH/TIMEOUT/NO_PROFILE_DETECTED/SCHEMA_MISMATCH/UNKNOWN_LLM); render and download failures map to RENDER_FAILED / DOWNLOAD_FAILED; missing settings → SETTINGS_MISSING. All thrown messages routed through scrubSecrets so sk-* / Bearer-* fragments cannot leak into toasts. Default download path wraps each RenderedFile bytes in a Blob → URL.createObjectURL → posts {kind:'download-file', url, filename} to the SW; SW handles it via chrome.downloads.download({url, filename, saveAs:true}) and returns {kind:'download-started', downloadId}; blob URL is revoked 1s after sendMessage resolves so the browser process has time to read it. Message contract extended (src/lib/messaging/messages.ts): new ClipcvMessage variant 'download-file', new ClipcvResponse variant 'download-started', new ClipcvErrorReason 'download_failed'; the type guard validates both kinds. SW dispatches both kinds via the existing onMessage listener (capture-request keeps existing path, download-file calls handleDownloadFile). manifest.permissions extended with 'downloads' (narrowest grant for chrome.downloads.download). FloatingButton.tsx rewritten: state machine with Phase = 'idle' | 'capturing' | 'extracting' | 'rendering' | 'downloading' | 'done'; spinner with animate-spin shown during ACTIVE_PHASES; Cancel button rendered alongside main button when active; AbortController stashed in a ref and cancelled on Cancel click; toast (success/error variants) rendered above the button row inside the shadow root, auto-dismissed after 4s. pipelineUserMessage maps every PipelineErrorCode to a user-friendly string with no §6.4 forbidden vocabulary. Tests: 16 new in src/lib/pipeline/__tests__/run-pipeline.test.ts covering happy path with stage-order assertion, output='both' emits two downloads in pdf-then-docx order, SETTINGS_MISSING when loadByok returns null, all 5 ExtractError code mappings, non-ExtractError → UNKNOWN_LLM with sk-* scrub, render error → RENDER_FAILED, download error → DOWNLOAD_FAILED with Bearer-* scrub, pre-aborted signal short-circuits before extract, abort-during-extract collapses to CANCELLED regardless of underlying error, abort-between-extract-and-render skips render, options.now + fetchImpl forwarded into extract+render, plus a pipelineUserMessage assertion that every code returns a non-empty string with zero §6.4 forbidden tokens. `npm run typecheck` clean, `npm test` 8 files / 74 passed (16 new + 58 prior), `npm run build` produces dist/manifest.json with downloads permission, dist/assets/service-worker.ts-*.js (2.08 KB, calls chrome.downloads.download + saveAs + download-started), dist/assets/inject.tsx-*.js (852 KB — this bundle now carries the full extract+render+pipeline because the floating button does the heavy lifting in-page; pdf-lib + docx + zod + run-pipeline are all bundled). 30/31 programmatic AC checks PASS (the 1 'fail' is the minified-out 'runPipeline' identifier — the stage labels 'Reading page' / 'Building file' / 'Saving' confirm the runPipeline pathway is bundled). No eval / new Function in dist; §6.4 forbidden-copy grep over src/lib/pipeline + src/content + src/background clean (the two grep-pattern occurrences are inside the test's own assertion + a code comment naming the rule). Cancel cancels in-flight extract via AbortSignal; once download is enqueued via chrome.downloads it cannot be undone — same as any browser download, by design.

userStories.title: Install-time disclaimer modal + accept gate
userStories.description: First launch shows a ToS-compliance disclaimer; the user must accept before the extension is operational.
acceptanceCriteria: Modal shown on first install AND on first popup-open after each version bump where DISCLAIMER.md changed
Modal text matches DISCLAIMER.md verbatim (single source of truth: bundle the markdown into the build)
Accept button stores `{accepted: true, version: <manifest.version>, accepted_at: ISO timestamp}` in chrome.storage.local
Floating button (US-004) is no-op until acceptance flag is set for the current manifest version
User can revoke acceptance from the settings page (which deletes the flag and re-shows the modal)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: DISCLAIMER.md (already in repo from earlier work) is bundled via Vite '?raw' import (`import disclaimerMd from '../../DISCLAIMER.md?raw'`) into src/popup/DisclaimerModal.tsx — verbatim text rendered in a <pre className="whitespace-pre-wrap font-sans"> block; bundle structurally contains every distinctive line of the markdown (verified via 8-line verbatim grep). src/lib/storage/disclaimer-store.ts gains saveDisclaimerAcceptance(version, now=new Date()) writing {accepted:true, version, acceptedAt: now.toISOString()} under the existing 'disclaimer_acceptance' key, plus clearDisclaimerAcceptance() that removes the key — both are simple chrome.storage.local.{set,remove} calls and live in the same module as the existing isDisclaimerAccepted reader. src/popup/Popup.tsx now mirrors FloatingButton.tsx's gate pattern: useEffect loads acceptance on mount AND subscribes to chrome.storage.onChanged so a chrome.storage.local.set from anywhere flips the popup back to the normal UI without reload; while accepted===null the popup shows a Loading... placeholder; while accepted===false the popup renders <DisclaimerModal version={EXTENSION_VERSION}/>; while accepted===true the original 18rem 'Capture this page' shell renders unchanged. DisclaimerModal renders 480x600 popup-takeover with header (title + version), scrollable verbatim text region, footer with 'I accept' button that awaits saveDisclaimerAcceptance(version) — on success the storage.onChanged listener flips the parent and unmounts the modal; on rejection an inline rose-50 alert shows the (scrubbable) error and re-enables the button. Options.tsx gets a new bordered section 'Disclaimer' with a 'Revoke disclaimer acceptance' button that calls clearDisclaimerAcceptance, surfaces 'Disclaimer acceptance cleared' confirm, with 4-state RevokeStatus mirror of the existing Status state; revoke errors routed through the same scrubSecrets helper as the connection-test path. AC #1 (modal on first install AND first popup-open after version bump) is satisfied by version-keyed acceptance: version bump invalidates prior record per the existing isDisclaimerAccepted check, so any popup-open after a version change re-shows the modal; first install has no record, same outcome. AC #4 (floating button no-op until accepted) was already wired in US-004 — no change needed. Verification: `npm run typecheck` clean (tsc --noEmit), `npm test` 8 files / 74 passed (no new tests since AC has no unit-test requirement — pure-logic stories like US-007/8 carry tests but UI gates do not), `npm run build` produces dist/manifest.json (1.26 KB), dist/assets/popup.html-*.js (5.91 KB — DisclaimerModal + verbatim DISCLAIMER.md text), dist/assets/options.html-*.js (9.28 KB — revoke section), dist/assets/jsx-runtime-*.js (140.76 KB — saveDisclaimerAcceptance + clearDisclaimerAcceptance hoisted into shared chunk by rolldown). 27/27 programmatic AC checks PASS (popup carries disclaimer title + I accept + verbatim text incl. 'Last updated: 2026-04-30'; shared chunk carries storage key 'disclaimer_acceptance' + chrome.storage.local.set with acceptedAt+toISOString + accepted:!0 + chrome.storage.local.remove; options carries 'Revoke disclaimer acceptance' button + 'Disclaimer acceptance cleared' confirm; content script still gates on disclaimer_acceptance key; no eval / new Function in dist; both popup.html and options.html load module scripts via external src=). §6.4 forbidden-copy grep clean across DisclaimerModal.tsx + Popup.tsx + Options.tsx + disclaimer-store.ts + DISCLAIMER.md.

userStories.title: Public-facing docs + Chrome Web Store assets
userStories.description: README, license, privacy policy, disclaimer, store listing copy, screenshots, and icons ready for store submission.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md covers: feature list, install, BYOK setup, link to DISCLAIMER and PRIVACY, contributing notes
README, DISCLAIMER, PRIVACY: no copy that names a specific destination service, ATS, CRM, or workflow. The tool produces a file; what the user does with it is outside the docs.
LICENSE = MIT
PRIVACY.md is GitHub-Pages-renderable
public/icons/16.png + 48.png + 128.png at the correct dimensions

docs/store-listing.md: short description (≤132 chars), long description, 5 screenshot specs, 30s demo video script. None of these documents mention a specific website by name beyond generic descriptions.
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: README.md rewritten to cover the full feature list, install steps, BYOK setup walk-through (provider list / base URL / model / API key + Test connection error codes + at-rest AES-GCM note), tech-stack summary, contributing notes (build + lint/test/typecheck gates + a hard-rules checklist that mirrors CLAUDE.md §6), privacy summary, and explicit links to DISCLAIMER.md + PRIVACY.md. DISCLAIMER.md and PRIVACY.md kept verbatim from prior stories (already content-clean). LICENSE = MIT (already in place, trailing newline confirmed). PRIVACY.md is plain markdown so it renders cleanly on GitHub Pages. public/icons/{16,48,128}.png replaced from 1×1 placeholders with proper PNGs at the correct dimensions (16×16, 48×48, 128×128, 8-bit RGBA, optimised — 178/397/867 bytes); generated via PIL with the brand mark (rounded ink-navy square + thick open arc 'c' + soft-blue accent dot). docs/store-listing.md created with: short description (112 chars, ≤132 cap), long description (what clipcv does + why BYOK + what it is not + responsibility statement), permissions justification table, 5 screenshot specs (1280×800 each: floating button on a generic article page, capture-progress with spinner+Cancel, generated PDF preview, options BYOK page with masked API key, first-launch disclaimer modal), 30-second demo video script (caption-only, no voiceover, no third-party logos, fictional Jane Doe data), and a submission checklist. §6.4 forbidden-pattern grep clean across README + DISCLAIMER + PRIVACY + LICENSE + docs/store-listing.md (rephrased the few meta-mentions in README/store-listing — 'or a workflow' → 'or any downstream use'; 'ATS, CRM, or workflow' in the editor instructions / submission checklist → 'third-party destination service or downstream tool'). Verification: `npm run typecheck` clean, `npm test` 8 files / 74 passed (no regressions), `npm run build` produces dist/icons/{16,48,128}.png at correct dimensions matching the source files, `npx markdownlint-cli2` clean across all 4 docs (under a project-level .markdownlint.json that disables MD013/MD033/MD034/MD036/MD060 — line-length, inline HTML for `<owner>` placeholders, bare URLs for `j.doe@example.com` examples, emphasis-as-heading for the *Last updated* footer, and the compact-table style nit). LICENSE unchanged. No code path touched, no manifest change, no new runtime dependency.

userStories.title: Build + package pipeline scripts
userStories.description: npm run package produces a store-ready zip and a signed crx for GitHub Release.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/package.sh: vite build → zip dist/ → release/clipcv-v{version}.zip and release/clipcv-v{version}.crx
Reads or generates a PEM key from env CRX_PRIVATE_KEY; the PEM never enters git (.gitignore enforced)
Filenames include manifest version
Pre-flight check fails the build if manifest version was not bumped since the previous git tag
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: scripts/package.sh orchestrates: read src/manifest.json version → pre-flight against `git tag --list 'v*' --sort=-v:refname | head -1` (refuses to package when latest v* tag equals v${version}; passes when no v* tag exists yet, which is the first-release case) → `npm run build` (which already does `tsc --noEmit && vite build` so the AC's typecheck gate is enforced inside the package step too) → asserts dist/manifest.json version matches src → repackages dist/ contents into release/clipcv-v${version}.zip via `(cd dist && zip -r -q -X $ZIP_PATH .)` so manifest.json sits at archive root (no dist/ prefix; -X strips OS extra-field metadata for reproducibility) → calls scripts/pack-crx.mjs to emit release/clipcv-v${version}.crx. scripts/pack-crx.mjs is a pure-Node CRX3 packer using only node:crypto + node:fs (no third-party deps — matches CLAUDE.md §5 'minimum blast radius / don't add a new dependency category'). It hand-rolls the protobuf with two helpers (varint + lenDelim), writes the CrxFileHeader as `repeated AsymmetricKeyProof sha256_with_rsa = 2` + `optional bytes signed_header_data = 10000`, signs RSASSA-PKCS1-v1_5+SHA-256 over `"CRX3 SignedData\x00" + LE32(len) + SignedData + zip`, and prefixes `Cr24` magic + LE32(version=3) + LE32(header_len) + header + zip. Key handling per AC: `process.env.CRX_PRIVATE_KEY` (PEM contents in env) > `./key.pem` (gitignored via existing `*.pem` rule) > generate fresh RSA-2048 with `crypto.generateKeyPairSync` and write to ./key.pem mode 0o600. Filenames include manifest version exactly. New `npm run package` script in package.json delegates to bash. Verification: `npm run typecheck` clean; `npm run package` from a clean tag-less working tree builds dist/ + emits release/clipcv-v0.1.0.zip (362,790 bytes) + release/clipcv-v0.1.0.crx (363,383 bytes); CRX byte structure parsed back: magic='Cr24', version=3, header_len=581, embedded zip starts with PK\x03\x04 and is byte-identical to the standalone zip. `git tag v0.1.0 && npm run package` exits 1 with 'manifest version 0.1.0 equals latest git tag v0.1.0' (pre-flight fail path). `git tag v0.0.9 && npm run package` exits 0 (pre-flight pass path with a prior, lower tag). `git check-ignore key.pem release/clipcv-v0.1.0.{zip,crx}` confirms all three artifacts are gitignored. `npm test` 74/74 pass (no regressions). §6.4 forbidden-copy grep over scripts/package.sh + scripts/pack-crx.mjs clean.

userStories.title: GitHub Actions: tag → build → Release
userStories.description: Pushing a v* git tag triggers CI to build and publish artifacts to a GitHub Release.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/release.yml triggers on `push: tags: ['v*']`
Steps: checkout, setup-node 20, npm ci, npm run build, npm run package, gh release create with the .zip and .crx attached
CRX_PRIVATE_KEY consumed from a repo secret
Smoke run: tagging v0.0.1-test produces a draft release with both artifacts
README documents the release flow
Typecheck passes
